title: Louisiana v. Callais
summary: The Supreme Court invalidated Louisiana's remedial second majority-Black congressional district and rewrote Section 2 redistricting doctrine to require stronger race-versus-politics and intentional-discrimination showings.
outcome_summary: Weakened Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act as a tool for challenging vote dilution in redistricting and reduced legal protection for Black voting power.
impact_notes: A major contemporary voting-rights rollback. The ruling did not formally strike Section 2 from the statute, but it raised the burden for plaintiffs and invalidated Louisiana's remedial second majority-Black district.

scores.notes: Major rollback of Section 2 redistricting enforcement without formally repealing the statute.

sources.source_title: Louisiana v. Callais, 608 U.S. ___ (2026) slip opinion
sources.publisher: Supreme Court of the United States
sources.notes: Official slip opinion PDF released by the Court.

sources.source_title: Louisiana v. Callais, Kagan dissent within slip opinion
sources.publisher: Supreme Court of the United States
sources.notes: The same official slip-opinion PDF includes Justice Kagan's dissent, which argues the ruling effectively guts Section 2's vote-dilution protection.

sources.source_title: Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act at the Supreme Court
sources.notes: Expert legal analysis explaining Section 2 doctrine, the Gingles framework, and the stakes of the Callais litigation.

sources.source_title: NAACP Condemns Supreme Court's Ruling Against Voting Rights
sources.notes: Civil-rights response describing the ruling as a major setback for Black voters and democratic participation.
